topic: Magnetic decay asymmetry ratio - c8 verification of ISS-2207 tldr_verdict wording patch
change_summary: c7 Hole 0 (minor): tldr_verdict opening clause was pedantically defender-exploitable in isolation. Decider self-applied ISS-2207: replaced opening 'The asymmetry ratio is reproduced by core-fluid-dynamics simulations' with 'The asymmetric decay is reproduced by core-fluid-dynamics simulations' to mirror detail_verdict_text framing (phenomenon / qualitative asymmetric decay). c8 verifies the patch landed cleanly and did not introduce new tensions.
our_argument_summary: WIN-063 verdict is Std Model Explains: the hemispheric magnetic dipole asymmetry phenomenon is reproduced qualitatively by MHD geodynamo simulations (Aubert et al. 2013) from independently measured CMB boundary conditions, but the specific 3:1 ratio is not first-principles in either model. The dome cites a 3:1 ratio with no published source and no derivation path from any of the four canonical dome parameters; the value is either post-hoc adoption or fiat assertion. Either path fails the independent-prediction test.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (no asymmetry-ratio domain present in raw-text snapshot)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automation: dome WIN-063 has no monitor.py entry per c7 verification - observation is asserted without programmatic check.
data_sources_used: CHAOS-7 / IGRF-14 cited prose-side; not wired into monitor.py
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: n/a - no automation to inspect for adaptive tolerance.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. The detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph already says no asymmetry-ratio domain appears in any monitor.py snapshot in our raw-text/ corpus - consistent with current observation.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No asymmetry-ratio domain in raw-text/monitor.py snapshot; confirmed unchanged from c7.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Geodynamo / reversed-flux-patch explanation (Aubert et al. 2013; Olson & Deguen 2012) explains hemispheric asymmetry qualitatively; dome renames the cause as toroidal aetheric return-path through the SAA shoulder without quantitative derivation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: 3:1 ratio published in CHAOS-7 / IGRF observational record predates any dome derivation; dome provides no derivation chain. Detail_verdict_text post-hoc paragraph already in place.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula connects any of the four canonical parameters (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, sun_altitude=5,733 km, moon_altitude=2,534 km) to specifically 3:1.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly notices a real empirical asymmetry in hemispheric dipole decay (data/wins.json:WIN-063.detail_evidence cites CHAOS-7 / Finlay et al. 2020 at doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9). Geodynamo physics does treat this asymmetry as a notable feature and explains it through reversed-flux-patch dynamics (Aubert et al. 2013, doi:10.1038/nature12574). The dome is right that the phenomenon is real and noteworthy.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Noticing an asymmetry is not predicting a specific ratio. The dome's claim is a 3:1 ratio, but data/wins.json:WIN-063.detail_evidence documents (in the dilemma paragraph) that no formula connects any of the four canonical dome parameters to specifically 3:1 over 2:1 or 5:1; no averaging window is specified; and the longitudinal-symmetry problem (the dome's H(r) is axisymmetric while the SAA sits at a specific longitude band) blocks the 'toroidal exit through SAA shoulder' mechanism from being a geometric prediction (data/wins.json:WIN-063.detail_evidence trailing paragraph). The verdict-text closer 'CHAOS-7 describes the field; geodynamo theory explains it; the dome merely relabels it' remains the load-bearing conclusion.

deeper_analysis: Verification scope: ISS-2207 was a single-word substitution in WIN-063.tldr_verdict opening clause ('asymmetry ratio is reproduced' -> 'asymmetric decay is reproduced'), targeting the c7 Hole 0 concern that a pedantic defender could quote the original first clause in isolation as self-contradictory (claiming the dome 3:1 ratio was reproduced) before reaching the qualifying clauses. The patch was applied verbatim per the c7 recommendation; tldr_verdict length grew by 1 char ('asymmetry'/9 -> 'asymmetric'/10, 'ratio'/5 -> 'decay'/5 wash on the second word, +1 net from the first-word swap). The new wording mirrors detail_verdict_text framing ('asymmetry phenomenon' / 'qualitative asymmetric decay emerges as a simulated output') - internal consistency restored. Defender-rehearsal of the c7 concern: a defender now reading the new first clause 'The asymmetric decay is reproduced by core-fluid-dynamics simulations from independently measured seismic-tomography inputs (Aubert et al. 2013)' in isolation can NOT honestly claim our verdict says the dome 3:1 prediction is reproduced - the phenomenon ('asymmetric decay') is what is reproduced, and the next clauses immediately note the specific 3:1 is not first-principles in either model and that the dome has no derivation path. The c7 concern is closed cleanly without weakening any argument; the dilemma structure (post-hoc adoption vs. fiat assertion -> either path fails the independent-prediction test) is fully preserved in subsequent clauses. No new defender-exploit surfaces introduced by the patch. Diff scope was confined to WIN-063.tldr_verdict (the remainder of the wins.json diff in commit e9f33bb is unicode-normalization artifacts on neighbor entries, not semantic change). Carry-over hole status (PROP-026 Phase 2 M3): c6 H2 (Olson & Deguen joint attribution in detail_evidence) was deferred at c7 pending network DOI verification and is not flagged as carry-forward in c7.unresolved_prior_cycle (empty array). Per the canonical convention I am following c7 - not re-promoting it without new information. open-issues.json contains zero items touching WIN-063, consistent with this verification confirming closure. Tests: c7 commit reported 3692/3692 pass; no subsequent commit has touched data/wins.json or build-script logic for WIN-063, so the test posture is unchanged. NARRATE-CITE compliance (PROP-014 Mech 3): all claims in kernel_of_truth cite either data/wins.json:WIN-063 fields or the actual DOI/URL anchors quoted in those fields.

citations_verified: data/wins.json:WIN-063.detail_evidence cites Finlay et al. 2020 CHAOS-7 at doi:10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 - DOI structure valid (verified at c7); confirmed unchanged in c8
data/wins.json:WIN-063.detail_evidence cites Aubert et al. 2013 at doi:10.1038/nature12574 - Nature DOI structure valid; confirmed unchanged in c8

summary_for_decider: WIN-063 c8 VERIFICATION CLEAN. ISS-2207 patch applied correctly: tldr_verdict opening 'The asymmetry ratio is reproduced' -> 'The asymmetric decay is reproduced'. New wording mirrors detail_verdict_text framing and removes the c7 H0 pedantic-isolation surface without weakening any argument. Verdict Std Model Explains holds. 0 new holes. recommended_action=no_change. No carry-forward. open-issues.json shows zero items touching WIN-063 - consistent with closure. c6 H2 (Olson & Deguen joint attribution) deferred again, not re-flagged as carry-forward. queue_id 407 set; decider can pop and archive. Singleton (verification class). Note for tinker: gate (d) of the Phase 1 verify-mode handoff strict-fails because ISS-2207 was same-run-resolved by the decider (no separate monitor/decisions/applied-patches/*.json file produced); per the literal language in curmudgeon.md the item stayed in curmudgeon scope, and this c8 confirms clean. If the operator intends same-run-resolved patches to also route to curmudgeon-verify, gate (d) needs to broaden to 'decider has applied a patch OR closed an ISS with closure_reason' since reviewed_at.
